story.title: Validation rules with inline warnings and undo-toast system replacing all alert() dialogs
story.as_a: Frigate user exporting YAML to a production config.yml
story.i_want: bad names, short polygons, and out-of-range coordinates flagged inline before export, and destructive actions to be instant-with-undo instead of alert dialogs
story.so_that: I never paste YAML that Frigate rejects at startup, and I never lose work to a misclick

acceptance_criteria: Given a zone with an empty name or a name failing /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/, when the user attempts Close, then the polygon is not committed, an inline error appears under the Name field, and no alert() fires
Given a zone name that exactly matches an existing camera name, when validated, then an inline error blocks commit with message explaining zones cannot share a camera's name
Given a polygon attempt with fewer than 3 points, when Close (button, Enter, or first-point click) is invoked, then an inline warning appears near the controls and the draft remains editable

Given any committed polygon whose normalized coordinate falls outside 0.0–1.0, when YAML regenerates, then a warning banner appears above the YAML panel naming the offending polygon (warn-only, export still allowed)

Given the user clicks delete on an item or Delete All, when the action fires, then it executes immediately and a 5-second toast with an Undo button appears; clicking Undo restores the exact prior state
Given zero validation issues, when the user works normally, then no warning UI elements are visible (no empty placeholders)
constraints: Zero alert()/confirm() calls remain in the file after this spec

Validation rules live in the Validate namespace as pure functions: Validate.polygonName(name, workspace), Validate.polygon(poly), Validate.workspace(ws) returning [{level:'error'|'warn', field, message}]
Errors block commit; warnings never block export (master spec: warn, don't block)
Toast/undo implemented with a snapshot of the affected slice (structuredClone), max one toast visible at a time, new toast finalizes the previous pending undo
Name regex per master spec is lowercase+underscore; Morgan extends to allow digits after first char since Frigate accepts them — flagged in defaults_applied
edge_cases: Auto-generated fallback names (item_1) must themselves pass the name regex
Undo clicked after the underlying camera/polygon list changed for other reasons — undo restores only the deleted entries by id, appended back at original index when possible
Motion masks and object masks have no Frigate-visible name; name rules apply as errors only to zones, as no-op for masks (name still used for list labels)
Rapid double-delete of two items — second toast supersedes first; first deletion is finalized, not lost
risks: Low: regex stricter than some working Frigate configs in the wild — warnings copy must say 'recommended by Frigate docs'
Low: toast stacking complexity — constrained to single-toast policy

open_questions.question: Should zone-name validation fire on keystroke or on commit?

options.label: On commit + live-clear once fixed (Recommended)
options.info: Avoids yelling at users mid-typing; error clears reactively when input becomes valid.

options.label: Live on every keystroke
options.info: Earliest feedback but flags every partially-typed name as invalid.

tasks.description: Implement Validate namespace pure rule functions (name regex, camera-name collision, min points, coordinate range)
Build inline warning components (field-level error text, YAML-panel banner) and CSS using existing --danger/--warning tokens
Build toast+undo system with state-slice snapshot; wire deletePolygon, clearPolygons, deleteCamera through it; remove all alert() calls
Wire Validate into Store.closePolygon (blocking) and Yaml regeneration (warn-only); verify all ACs incl. zero-alert grep

docs_md:
## Validation & Warnings
Pure-function validation rules in the Validate namespace, surfaced as inline field errors (blocking) and YAML-panel banners (non-blocking). All alert()/confirm() removed; deletes become instant with a 5s undo toast, honoring master spec UI principle #6.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Pure validation rules — also reused verbatim by YAML import (spec 06)

metrics: 0 occurrences of alert( or confirm( in the file (grep check)
100% of the 6 master-spec validation rules covered by a Validate function with a passing manual test case each
Undo restores deleted items with identical ids and order in 100% of QA undo scenarios

decision_log.rationale: Validation must land before import (spec 06) and point editing (spec 05) because both reuse the same rule functions; toast/undo must land before persistence so destructive actions are safe once work is durable.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: validate at export time only — user discovers errors after drawing 10 polygons
Rejected: confirm() dialogs for deletes — explicitly banned by master spec UI principle #6
Rejected: full undo/redo stack — out of v0.2 scope; single-slot undo covers the destructive cases
tradeoffs: Single-toast policy means a fast second delete finalizes the first silently
Name regex slightly looser than master spec's /^[a-z_]+$/ to match real Frigate behavior
decision_log.confidence_rationale: Rules are enumerated in the master spec; UI surface is small and additive.

defaults_applied: Extended master-spec name regex /^[a-z_]+$/ to /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/ because Frigate accepts digits in zone names — override if strict spec-regex is required
Assuming warnings render in English only; no i18n
Assuming coordinate-range check is warn-only since the canvas cannot normally produce out-of-range points (guard per master spec)
